title: Protein extraction form Aurantiochytrium limacinum (ATCC MYA-1381)
author: Anbarasu Karthikaichamy

steps_raw.instruction: Start by making stocks of the following, 1 Molarity (M) KCl 25 millimolar (mM) MgCl 2 1 Molarity (M) Tris Prepare lysis buffer (LyB) by adding the following chemicals (for 10ml), %s %s
Pipette out ~3 ml of cell suspension from the test tube or conica flask. Centrifuge the cells at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes Discard the supernatant and keep the cell pellet on ice.

Add 1 ml of lysis buffe (LyB) to the pellet. Vortex the mixture for 20 mins on high spped. Centrifuge the suspension at 13500 rpm, 4 °C, 15 minutes Collect the supernatant in a separate tube and place it on ice. This is the soluble fraction.

Resuspend the pellet from the above step in 4x LDS sample buffer Add required amount of dH 2 O Add 1:10(v:v) 25% β-Mercaptoethanol Always handle β-Mercaptoethanol in fume hood! Heat the mixture at 70 °C for 00:10:00 Centrifuge the mixture at 13500 rpm, 4 °C, 20 minutes Carefully aspirate the supernatant (insoluble fraction) into a fresh tube and keep it on ice. Alternatively, one could also use custom buffers (with high concentration of detergent) for insoluble fraction extraction.
Quantify the protein extract using an appropriate mehtod and the proteins can be visualized on a SDS-PAGE gel stained with coomassie blue.
